ksas.statement: Ability to make defensible architectural decisions under uncertainty, documenting the rationale, rejected alternatives, and conditions that would trigger re-evaluation.
Knowledge of data modeling techniques including conceptual, logical, and physical modeling, entity-relationship modeling, dimensional modeling, and data vault methodology.
Knowledge of data platform architecture patterns, including data warehouse, data lake, data lakehouse, data mesh, and data fabric architectures, and the trade-offs each presents.
Knowledge of data integration patterns including ETL, ELT, event streaming, CDC (change data capture), API-based integration, and data virtualization.
Knowledge of cloud data platform services across major providers, including storage, compute, streaming, and orchestration capabilities and their architectural implications.
Knowledge of data classification, sensitivity labeling, and the architectural controls required to enforce data access, privacy, and residency requirements by design.
Skill in producing enterprise data architecture artifacts, including data flow diagrams, entity-relationship models, data domain maps, and integration architecture documentation.
Skill in evaluating data platform technology options against requirements, including performance, scalability, cost, security, and operational complexity trade-offs.
Skill in defining and enforcing enterprise data modeling standards, naming conventions, and structural patterns across engineering teams.
Design and document the enterprise data architecture, including platform topology, data domain structure, integration patterns, and data lifecycle policies.
Review and approve data model designs produced by data engineers and analysts, ensuring conformance with enterprise standards and architectural principles.
Evaluate and recommend data platform technology selections, producing architecture decision records with evidence-based rationale.
Define enterprise data integration standards, including interface contracts, schema evolution policies, API versioning standards, and event schema governance.
Ability to evaluate build-versus-buy decisions for ML platform components with clear criteria based on organizational maturity, team capability, and long-term operational cost.
Knowledge of ML platform components including feature stores, model registries, experiment tracking systems, model serving infrastructure, and pipeline orchestration frameworks.
Knowledge of MLOps principles and practices, including CI/CD for ML, automated retraining pipelines, model versioning, A/B testing infrastructure, and canary deployment patterns.
Knowledge of cloud AI/ML platform services (e.g., SageMaker, Vertex AI, Azure ML) and the architectural patterns for building portable versus cloud-native ML infrastructure.
Knowledge of GPU and accelerated compute infrastructure, including cluster management, resource scheduling, distributed training architectures, and cost optimization strategies.
Skill in designing ML training and serving infrastructure that scales from prototype to production without requiring architectural rework.
Skill in designing feature engineering pipelines that produce consistent, reproducible, low-latency features for both batch and real-time model serving.
Design and document the enterprise ML platform architecture, including training infrastructure, feature store design, model registry, serving layer, and monitoring stack.
Define MLOps standards and pipeline templates that enable data science teams to move models to production without bespoke engineering effort for each use case.
Evaluate and select ML platform tooling, producing architectural decision records with documented rationale and re-evaluation criteria.
Ability to diagnose and resolve data pipeline failures under time pressure, with clear incident documentation and root cause analysis.
Knowledge of data pipeline design patterns, including batch processing, streaming processing, micro-batch, and lambda and kappa architecture trade-offs.
Knowledge of distributed data processing frameworks (e.g., Spark, Flink, Beam) and when each is appropriate for a given data volume, latency, and complexity requirement.
Knowledge of data storage technologies, including columnar storage formats (Parquet, ORC), transactional databases, NoSQL stores, object storage, and their appropriate use cases.
Knowledge of data quality validation techniques and how to embed quality checks into pipeline execution with appropriate failure handling and alerting.
Knowledge of data pipeline orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow, Prefect, Dagster) and how to design DAGs that are observable, recoverable, and maintainable.
Skill in writing production-quality data transformation code in SQL and at least one general-purpose programming language (Python or Scala), including unit testing and documentation.
Skill in designing and implementing data pipeline monitoring, including lineage tracking, data freshness alerting, volume anomaly detection, and schema drift detection.
Skill in performance tuning data pipelines, including query optimization, partition strategy, caching, and resource scaling.
Design, build, and maintain data ingestion pipelines from source systems to the data platform, including schema mapping, transformation logic, and error handling.
Implement and maintain data quality validation checks within pipelines, documenting quality rules, threshold definitions, and remediation procedures.
Optimize existing data pipelines for performance, cost, and reliability, documenting changes and their measured impact.
Collaborate with data architects and analysts to translate data consumption requirements into pipeline designs and storage structures.
Ability to identify and resolve metric inconsistency problems that arise when different teams define the same business concept differently in their own queries.
Knowledge of dimensional modeling principles, including star schema, snowflake schema, slowly changing dimensions, and the design trade-offs between normalization and query performance.
Knowledge of dbt (data build tool) or equivalent transformation framework patterns, including model layering (staging, intermediate, mart), testing, documentation, and lineage generation.
Knowledge of metrics layer concepts, including semantic layer design, metric definition standards, and how centralized metric definitions prevent inconsistency across reporting tools.
Skill in writing well-structured, tested, and documented SQL transformations that produce reliable, reusable datasets for analytics consumers.
Skill in translating business metric definitions from domain stakeholders into precise, unambiguous SQL logic with documented assumptions and edge case handling.
Design and maintain the analytics transformation layer, including staging models, intermediate transformations, and consumption-ready data mart models.
Define, document, and implement enterprise metric definitions in the semantic layer, ensuring consistency across BI tools and analytical consumers.
Implement automated data testing on all transformation outputs, including uniqueness, not-null, referential integrity, and custom business rule tests.

Knowledge of cloud data platform infrastructure services, including managed compute, serverless processing, object storage, data warehouse, and streaming platform options across major cloud providers.
Knowledge of infrastructure-as-code principles and tooling (e.g., Terraform, Pulumi) as applied to data platform provisioning and configuration management.
Knowledge of data platform security controls, including network isolation, encryption at rest and in transit, identity and access management, and audit logging.
Skill in deploying, configuring, and scaling data platform components using infrastructure-as-code with version control and automated testing.
Skill in implementing data platform cost management practices, including resource tagging, usage monitoring, cost allocation, and rightsizing recommendations.
Provision, configure, and maintain the data platform infrastructure, including compute environments, storage systems, networking, and security controls.
Implement platform monitoring and alerting for availability, performance, cost, and security events, with defined escalation procedures.
Manage data platform access controls, including role definitions, permission grants, access reviews, and privilege escalation procedures.

Knowledge of ML pipeline orchestration tools and patterns, including feature stores, experiment tracking, model registries, and the architectural trade-offs between managed and self-hosted ML platforms.

Knowledge of model deployment strategies, including batch inference, real-time serving, edge deployment, A/B testing, canary releases, and shadow deployments, and the infrastructure requirements and failure modes of each.

Knowledge of model monitoring requirements, including data drift detection, prediction drift, performance degradation metrics, and the alerting thresholds and retraining triggers appropriate for different model types and serving patterns.

Knowledge of containerization and infrastructure-as-code as applied to ML workloads, including GPU resource management, distributed training job scheduling, and reproducible environment management across development and production.

Skill in designing and implementing CI/CD pipelines for ML models, including automated testing at unit, integration, and model quality levels, artifact versioning, and deployment automation with rollback capability.

Skill in diagnosing and resolving ML infrastructure failures, including training job crashes, serving latency spikes, resource contention across shared clusters, and data pipeline breakdowns that affect model inputs.
Build and maintain automated ML pipelines from feature engineering through model training, validation, deployment, and monitoring, ensuring reproducibility and auditability at every stage.

Implement model monitoring and observability systems that detect data drift, prediction drift, and performance degradation, triggering alerts or automated retraining workflows when defined thresholds are exceeded.

Knowledge of DataOps principles and practices, including pipeline automation, environment management, data testing strategies, configuration-as-code, and the distinction between DataOps, DevOps, and MLOps as complementary disciplines.
Knowledge of data pipeline orchestration tools and scheduling patterns, including dependency graph management, failure handling, retry strategies, idempotency requirements, and SLA-driven monitoring.

Knowledge of data quality testing frameworks, including schema validation, data contract enforcement, freshness checks, volume anomaly detection, and referential integrity verification within automated pipelines.

Skill in implementing automated data pipeline testing — unit tests for individual transformations, integration tests for end-to-end data flows, and regression tests that validate output consistency across pipeline changes.

Skill in designing data pipeline observability systems, including lineage tracking, execution monitoring, cost attribution, and root-cause analysis tooling for pipeline failures and data quality incidents.

Build and maintain automated data pipelines with version-controlled transformations, automated testing, environment parity between development and production, and deployment processes that enable rapid iteration without production risk.
Implement and operate data observability infrastructure that provides pipeline lineage, execution history, data quality metrics, and proactive alerting for anomalies and SLA violations.

Manage data environment provisioning and configuration, ensuring development, staging, and production environments maintain parity and that pipeline promotions follow defined testing and approval workflows.
